meta.title: Protestant-Orthodox Relationship: The Moment That Defined It
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: At the 1519 Leipzig Debate, who argued that all Eastern Orthodox Christians are damned?
questions.answer: Johann Eck

questions.question: How did Luther respond to the claim that all Eastern Orthodox Christians are damned?
options: He said nothing more detestable than this blasphemy could be spoken
He proposed a formal council to resolve the question
questions.answer: He said nothing more detestable than this blasphemy could be spoken

questions.question: Where were the Lutheran theologians who corresponded with Patriarch Jeremiah II located?
questions.answer: Tübingen

questions.question: What document did the Tübingen Lutherans send to Patriarch Jeremiah II?
questions.answer: The Augsburg Confession

questions.question: How many letters did each side send in the Tübingen–Constantinople correspondence?
questions.answer: Three each

questions.question: In what decades did the correspondence between the Tübingen Lutherans and Jeremiah II take place?
What title does the Archbishop of Constantinople hold?
questions.answer: Ecumenical Patriarch

questions.question: Why does Ortlund say the Protestant–Orthodox relationship is especially interesting compared to other inter-Christian relationships?
options: They have the most theological disagreements
They lack the direct contact and shared history that other traditions have
They were formally unified until the 20th century
questions.answer: They lack the direct contact and shared history that other traditions have

questions.question: What did the Lutherans express openness to in the opening of their first letter?
questions.answer: God working on both sides

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT an area of agreement between the Lutherans and Jeremiah II?
questions.answer: The Filioque clause

questions.question: What did Jeremiah II acknowledge at the end of his second letter?
options: That the Lutheran position was superior
That they had agreed on almost all of the main subjects
That the Orthodox Church needed reform
That the correspondence should continue indefinitely
questions.answer: That they had agreed on almost all of the main subjects

questions.question: Which theological disagreement does Ortlund identify as perhaps the biggest one between the two sides?
options: The nature and infallibility of the Church
questions.answer: The nature and infallibility of the Church

questions.question: What is the Filioque clause about?
options: Whether Christ has one nature or two
Whether the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father alone or also from the Son
Whether baptism should be by immersion or pouring
questions.answer: Whether the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father alone or also from the Son

questions.question: In Ortlund's tree metaphor, what represents the shared theological foundation between Protestants and Orthodox?
questions.answer: The trunk

questions.question: How did Jeremiah II address the Lutherans in his first letter?
options: As spiritual sons of his humble self
As ambassadors of the Western Church
questions.answer: As spiritual sons of his humble self

questions.question: What did Jeremiah II demand in his second letter?
options: That the Lutherans put aside their innovations and enter the Orthodox Church
That the Lutherans accept the Filioque
That the pope mediate the dispute
questions.answer: That the Lutherans put aside their innovations and enter the Orthodox Church

questions.question: How did Jeremiah II end the correspondence in his third letter?
options: By requesting they stop writing about dogmas and write only for friendship
By accepting some Lutheran positions as valid
questions.answer: By requesting they stop writing about dogmas and write only for friendship

questions.question: What is Ortlund's colloquial summary of Jeremiah's three letters?
options: 'I'm happy to help, You guys have issues, Stop talking to me'
questions.answer: 'I'm happy to help, You guys have issues, Stop talking to me'

questions.question: How did the Lutherans respond after Jeremiah cut off the dialogue?
options: They issued a formal condemnation of the Orthodox Church
They acknowledged his request but expressed hope for future dialogue and offered friendship and prayers
They immediately sent a fourth letter demanding a response
They appealed to the pope to intervene
questions.answer: They acknowledged his request but expressed hope for future dialogue and offered friendship and prayers

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what does Jeremiah's vision of Christian unity require?
options: Institutional incorporation into the Orthodox Church
questions.answer: Institutional incorporation into the Orthodox Church

questions.question: What is the Lutheran criterion for Christian unity as expressed in their letters?
options: Submission to a single ecclesiastical authority
Adherence to the gospel of Christ as held forth in Scripture
Acceptance of all seven ecumenical councils
questions.answer: Adherence to the gospel of Christ as held forth in Scripture

questions.question: What does the word 'irenicism' mean as Ortlund uses it?
options: Doctrinal compromise for the sake of unity
Aiming for peace in theological dialogue
Accepting every tradition as equally valid
questions.answer: Aiming for peace in theological dialogue

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what two opposite errors do many Protestants fall into?
questions.answer: Feisty sectarianism and squishy ecumenism

questions.question: What did the Lutherans claim about their doctrine in response to the charge of innovation?
options: That it was a necessary modern update
That it was founded upon the Word of God and not new
That it was a return to pre-Nicene Christianity
That it was a synthesis of Eastern and Western thought
questions.answer: That it was founded upon the Word of God and not new

questions.question: Which comparison does Ortlund use to show that Protestants and Orthodox share more common ground than other interfaith pairs?
options: Christian and Mormon, who don't share the Trinity and the God-man
questions.answer: Christian and Mormon, who don't share the Trinity and the God-man

questions.question: According to Ortlund, does affirming true Christians outside of Protestantism compromise any mainstream Protestant confession?
options: Yes, it contradicts the Westminster Confession
Yes, it violates the Augsburg Confession
No, it does not compromise any mainstream Protestant standard
It depends on the specific denomination
questions.answer: No, it does not compromise any mainstream Protestant standard

questions.question: What key medieval Western soteriological concepts did both the Lutherans and Jeremiah II reject?
options: Indulgences, purgatory, and the treasury of merit
Icons, relics, and prayer to saints
questions.answer: Indulgences, purgatory, and the treasury of merit

questions.question: Jeremiah II's ecclesiology, as described by Ortlund, held that the Church is characterized by what quality?
questions.answer: Infallibility — the Church cannot err

questions.question: What is Ortlund's concluding appeal to Protestant viewers?
options: To reclaim the Protestant heritage of irenicism — conviction and openness together
To adopt the Orthodox liturgical calendar
questions.answer: To reclaim the Protestant heritage of irenicism — conviction and openness together

questions.question: Which key Lutheran quote does Ortlund hold up as a model at the end of the video?
options: 'Here I stand; I can do no other'
'We desire from the bottom of our hearts to preserve a God-pleasing peace with all who love the gospel of Christ'
questions.answer: 'We desire from the bottom of our hearts to preserve a God-pleasing peace with all who love the gospel of Christ'
